goal: rc5: Conflict detection on sync + seal, auto-before-command sync wrapper with freshness window, status sync-staleness, AGENTS.md v3 simplification, mainline merge demoted in docs. Implements all 6 patches of mainline-spec-v0.1-rc5-patch.md in one PR.

summary.title: rc5: conflict detection on sync+seal, auto-before-command sync, status staleness, AGENTS.md v3, merge demoted in docs
summary.what: Implements all 6 patches of mainline-spec-v0.1-rc5-patch.md in one branch. New domain types (ConflictPair, PartialFingerprint, LastSync) and a fresh internal/engine/conflict.go module that scores both freshly-sealed full fingerprints and active-draft partial fingerprints against the materialised view. Service.Sync now writes a last-sync record and emits NewConflicts after computing a 'new since prior view' delta; Service.SealSubmitWithOptions auto-runs the same sync + a phase1 check on the just-sealed fingerprint, with a new --offline flag that skips both. Service.Status surfaces last-sync timing and a SyncStale boolean. CLI gains: a persistent --no-sync flag plus a PersistentPreRun wrapper that auto-syncs (subject to a freshness window) before pin / reconcile / list-proposals / context / check; Status prints a Sync row; sync prints NewConflicts; seal prints conflicts and --offline; merge --help carries a long NOTE pointing users at the GitHub PR + auto-pin path. AGENTS.md (both the docs file and the engine.go init template) bumped to v3 — agents no longer call sync/pin themselves.
summary.why: rc5 patch motivated by dogfood data: 13 pinned intents in this repo, all via=pin, none via merge — Service.Merge has 0% real-world usage. Conflict detection only ever fired when the reviewer explicitly ran `mainline check`, which is too late. Sync was a manual command users forgot to run, so the view drifted. Together the gap meant the central product promise (catch intent conflicts early) was not delivered. rc5 closes the gap by automating the sync-and-check at the two natural moments — when an author seals an intent and when anyone pulls fresh state — without forcing a merge-command rewrite on teams already happy with GitHub PRs.
summary.user_goal: Make 'detect conflicts before they hit main' actually true in the default workflow without changing how teams merge.

decisions.chose: Hardcode in the cli package (rootCmd.PersistentPreRun + autoSyncCommands map)
decisions.rationale: It's a product behaviour, not a team policy. Leaving it in config invites teams to silently disable conflict detection by editing one line. Freshness window stays in config because that one *does* depend on team latency tolerance.

Seal is a user commitment; tools should not refuse to record a commitment because of a heuristic match. Reviewers and the agent both still see the warning via JSON and stdout. Forcing block invites --force escape hatches that are worse.

decisions.chose: Sum of files_touched + lowercased keyword tokens (len ≥ 4, stopword-filtered) + path-derived subsystems
decisions.rationale: Drafts cannot honestly populate architectural_claims or behavioral_changes; trying to invent them would create fake high-confidence matches. Files + words + path subsystem is what the draft genuinely knows.
rejected: Stem and lemmatise tokens — adds a dependency for marginal recall improvement.
Run an LLM over goal+turns to extract architectural claims — defeats the point of fast partial scoring.

decisions.chose: Hardcoded constant 0.25 in conflict.go, separate from cfg.Check.Phase1Threshold
decisions.rationale: Partial fingerprints have systematically less signal — they need a different floor than full ones. Surfacing two thresholds in config would muddy the existing one. The constant is documented inline alongside the rationale.

decisions.chose: Compare pre-rebuild view's intent IDs vs post-rebuild; only warn on remote pairs whose ID is new
decisions.rationale: Without this, every sync re-warns about every previously-known overlap — noise that drowns the actual signal. First-ever sync (priorIDs empty) suppresses the warning entirely; later syncs surface only what just appeared.
Existing callers (engine_test.go, integration tests, the SDK adapter) keep compiling. The CLI's new --offline flag goes through the options path.

decisions.chose: Long help text rewritten to explain it's advanced; not hidden, not deprecated
decisions.rationale: Spec called for keeping the command but moving it out of the main path — exactly what cobra's Long does. Hiding it would break automation that already depends on it; deprecating would imply removal which we don't plan.

risks: Seal's auto-sync inserts ~3s of network latency into every seal --submit on a remote-attached repo. Acceptable because seal is itself a low-frequency end-of-task action; mitigated by --offline for users who want zero-network seal.

Partial fingerprint threshold 0.25 is a guess — not validated against real conflict samples. May be loud or quiet in practice; tunable later via the same kind of dogfood + grid search rc4 prescribed for the full-fingerprint threshold.

Sync's delta filter relies on the cached view existing pre-sync. If a user wipes .ml-cache/views/mainline.json between syncs, the next sync sees an empty prior view → no auto-check fires. Documented in the Sync function comment; users would need to re-trigger via the seal path.
followups: Calibrate partialFingerprintThreshold once we have ≥30 real intent pairs across teams.
v0.2: GitHub Action that runs `mainline pin` on PR-merge events → no agent or human sync-and-pin step needed at all.
Audit `thread` command for the same 'name vs function' mismatch that prompted reconcile→pin.
Add a `mainline check --history <intent>` companion to LastCheck (deferred from PR #12).

architectural_claims: Conflict detection lives in a single engine module (conflict.go) consumed by both Sync and SealSubmit; CLI never re-implements scoring.
Auto-sync timing is driven by a hardcoded command list plus a configurable freshness window; product behaviour vs team policy split is explicit.
Seal's --offline path is the sole bypass for the auto sync+check loop; all other paths go through it.
behavioral_changes: mainline sync writes .ml-cache/views/last-sync.json and surfaces NewConflicts in stdout/JSON.
mainline seal --submit now auto-syncs and runs phase1 conflict detection unless --offline.
mainline status shows last-sync age and a stale flag (>24h by default).
mainline pin / reconcile / check / list-proposals / context auto-sync if last sync is >5 min old.
mainline merge --help carries a long NOTE explaining it's not the default path.
AGENTS.md no longer instructs agents to call sync or pin.

api_changes.signature: engine.SyncResult gains NewSealedSeen and NewConflicts; engine.SealSubmitResult gains SyncRan/SyncError/Conflicts; engine.StatusResult gains LastSync/SyncStaleSeconds/SyncStale

data_model_changes.name: domain.ConflictPair / domain.PartialFingerprint / domain.CheckSummary already existed; added domain.LastSync at .ml-cache/views/last-sync.json
data_model_changes.migration_notes: First Sync after upgrade writes the file; absence is treated as never-synced (stale).

security_implications: auto-sync triggered by routine commands now performs git fetch on the user's behalf. Same trust boundary as before — just more frequent.
